title: Exodeenian

{{Quote|You should have taken the precautions up front. I don't know how I'll tell the people of Exodeen that one of their most beloved figures is dead.|Senator R'yet Coome, after the death of Senator M'yet Luure|<ref name="TNR" />}}
The '''Exodeenians''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] beings native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Exodeen/Legends|Exodeen]]. They had six [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and six legs, as well as six sets of [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. They joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] during the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansion Era]], circa [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]]. Their world's [[Exodeen system/Legends|star system]] [[Battle of the Exodeen system (Mandalorian Wars)|fell]] to the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] during the [[Mandalorian Wars]] and was under the control of the [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Rejoining the Republic following these conflicts, the Exodeenians later joined its successor, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Although some Exodeenians served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Exodeen itself was an Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] by [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]].

Shortly after, in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], Exodeen joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], represented in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|Senate]] by Exodeenian [[M'yet Luure]] and two others. Luure successfully advocated to allow former Imperial functionaries to serve in the Senate. The Exodeenian [[R'yet Coome]] benefited from this and became the junior [[Senator/Legends|senator]] from Exodeen. As part of a political faction composed of former Imperials, Coome almost arranged a [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|vote of no confidence]] in [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|President]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] following the [[Almanian Uprising|detonation]] of [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] in the Senate chamber. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], the Exodeenian [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] [[Battle of the Exodeen system (Yuuzhan Vong War)|fell]] to the extra-[[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] invaders.

==Biology and appearance==
The Exodeenians were a non-[[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]], [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Exodeen/Legends|Exodeen]]. They had six [[Limb/Legends|arms]], six legs and a long tail; consequently, they had difficulties fitting in regulation chairs designed for bipedal species, such as those in the [[Senate Building/Legends|Imperial Senate Building]]. Furthermore, they had six rows of uneven [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]]. Some [[Human/Legends|Humans]], such as [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]], could not tell individual Exodeenians apart by sight. Exodeenians could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], although they did so with the distinctive sound as their voice filtered through six sets of teeth.<ref name="TNR" />

==Society and culture==
Exodeenians developed intricate etiquette based on their six-armed physique. For instance, a touch on an Exodeenian's first arm was a signal to cease speaking, while a touch on the second arm was a challenge to fight. Some non-Exodeenians were familiar with this etiquette, such as [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Meido]] of [[Adin]]. Other Exodeenian mannerisms included resting their lower hands on their secondary hips or preening their six arms.<ref name="TNR" />

Exodeenians ate a meal known as [[Exodeenian pasta]], which could also be found served at the [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid belt]] [[Smuggler's Run/Legends|Smuggler's Run]].<ref name="TNR" />

==History==
[[File:Exodeen.jpg|thumb|right|A map of Exodeen and surrounding systems in the Colonies]]

===Galactic Republic===
The Exodeenians evolved on the planet Exodeen,<ref name="TNR" /> located within [[The Slice/Legends|the Slice]] portion of the [[Colonies/Legends|Colonies]]. It was positioned on the [[Brentaal–Denon Route|Brentaal-Denon Route]], which later became incorporated into the [[Hydian Way/Legends|Hydian Way]]—the only [[Hyperspace route/Legends|super-hyperroute]] to span the entire length of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].Exodeen also connected the Hydian with the [[Quellor Run/Legends|Quellor Run]] via a hyperlane branching off to [[Quellor/Legends|Quellor]]. The Exodeenians joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] circa [[22,000 BBY/Legends|22,000 BBY]], during a period known as the [[Expansionist Period/Legends|Expansionist Era]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

When the Republic was [[Mandalorian Wars|invaded]] by the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] in [[3962 BBY]], the [[Exodeen system/Legends|Exodeen system]], which contained the Exodeenian homeworld, [[Battle of the Exodeen system (Mandalorian Wars)|fell]] to the invaders as part of their [[Mandalorian Triumph|successful push]] into the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]], which came to a halt when they captured the planet of [[Duro/Legends|Duro]]. The Republic, newly led by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Darth Malak|Malak]], rolled back all the Mandalorian gains, eventually defeating them at the [[Battle of Malachor V]] in [[3960 BBY]]. However, this conflict led Revan and Malak to turn the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and within a year, they invaded the Republic at the head of a [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire|Sith Empire]]. Following numerous successes, this Empire controlled vast swaths of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] and [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]], as well as discrete areas further coreward, including the Exodeenian homeworld. The Republic defeated the Sith Empire in [[3951 BBY]], following which the Republic slowly began reintegrating its lost territory.<ref name="TEA" />

Exodeen remained within Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]] during two other galactic wars, the [[New Sith Wars]] of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] and the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] of [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" />

===Galactic Empire===
Following the Clone Wars, [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] declared a new [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="NEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> which the Exodeenians joined;<ref name="TNR" /> however, due to the Empire's [[humanocentrism]], Exodeen was considered unimportant by the Empire as it was occupied by non-humanoids.<ref name="GSWU">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> While some Exodeenians served as minor functionaries for the Empire, others, such as [[M'yet Luure]], served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]],<ref name="TNR" /> a resistance movement trying to overthrow what it perceived to be an illegitimate Empire.<ref name="NEC" /> The Empire heavily fortified Exodeen, and it was considered an Imperial [[Fortress world/Legends|fortress world]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]]. It remained under Imperial control through to [[8 ABY/Legends|8 ABY]], despite the fact that the Alliance's successor, the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], gained control of much of the surrounding core regions of the galaxy during this time.<ref name="TEA" />

===New Republic===
{{Quote|It seems to me that this government is already being torn apart. We need leadership. Good leadership would have prevented this attack.|Senator R'yet Coome after the death of Senator M'yet Luure|<ref name="TNR" />}}
[[File:Bombed_NR_Senate_Hall.jpg|thumb|left|upright|Exodeenian senator M'yet Luure was killed during the Senate bombing of 17 ABY. The aftermath is pictured here.]]
By [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], Exodeen had switched sides and joined the New Republic.<ref name="TEA" /> As of [[17 ABY/Legends|17 ABY]], they were represented in the [[New Republic Senate/Legends|New Republic Senate]] by three [[Senator/Legends|senators]]. Luure, a former member of the Alliance, was their senior senator, wielding considerable influence among his peers. He strongly argued for allowing former Imperial bureaucrats to serve in the New Republic Senate, swaying the Senate with his arguments against such opponents as Leia Organa Solo. This law change allowed fellow Exodeenian [[R'yet Coome]] to serve as the junior senator from Exodeen; Coome had previously been barred from serving in the Senate because of his time spent working for the Empire, albeit in a minor position.<ref name="TNR" />

At the first such Senate meeting with newly-appointed former Imperials, the Senate Building was [[Bombing of the Senate Hall|rocked]] by explosions from [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] placed inside many of the senators' [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droids]]. The explosions [[Death/Legends|killed]] many planetary representatives, including Luure and the [[Unidentified Exodeen secondary senator|secondary senator]] from Exodeen. Of the Exodeenian delegation, only junior senator Coome survived. Two days after the explosion, [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|President]] Organa Solo called an informal meeting of the surviving senators, informing them of the progress of an investigation into the explosion. At this meeting, Coome formed a faction along with a number of other junior senators, such as Meido of Adin and [[Wwebyls]] of [[Yn]], passing a motion to run a second, independent, investigation into the cause of the explosion. Coome was later elected to the [[Inner Council (New Republic)|Inner Council]] of the Senate. At a meeting of the Council, hastily called by Senator Meido in order to throw Organa Solo off-balance, Meido accused her husband, [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], of being behind the bombs, based on the preliminary findings from their report. The Exodeenian junior senator Coome followed this accusation up with a call for a warrant to be issued for Solo's arrest.<ref name="TNR" />

Coome, along with Meido and Wwebyls, was responsible for leaking their findings to the wider Senate in a bid to pass a [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|vote of no confidence]] in Organa Solo's leadership. However, the day before the vote was due to occur, Organa Solo was personally threatened by [[Kueller]], a former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] trainee and the ruler of [[Almania]]. He claimed responsibility for the Senate bombing and [[Almanian Uprising|demanded]] she turn control of the Republic over to him. Kueller held Organa Solo's brother, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], [[Prisoner/Legends|hostage]] in order to force her to comply. Organa Solo resigned her position to break his leverage and rendered the forthcoming vote irrelevant. After Organa Solo personally helped defeat Kueller, Coome and the faction he was part of backed down entirely, allowing Organa Solo to be restored to her position as President of the Republic.<ref name="TNR" />

Later, in [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the New Republic was invaded by an extra-galactic species known as the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. They conquered significant portions of the galaxy, [[Battle of the Exodeen system (Yuuzhan Vong War)|capturing]] Exodeen during a push into the Core. They controlled the Exodeenian homeworld until at least [[27 ABY/Legends|62:4 GrS]], but their galactic invasion was finally defeated in [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]]. More than a century later, the planet Exodeen was part of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]].<ref name="TEA" />

==Exodeenians in the galaxy==
{{Quote|I'll be lucky if the human dies within the first ten kilometers, I've got two thousand riding on it. Cross your fingers for me.|Haari Ikreme Beeerd discusses Luke Skywalker|<ref name="RFT">''[[Rebel Force: Target]]''</ref>}}
In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], an Exodeenian was present on [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] in the support tent for [[Podracer/Legends|podracers]]; also in the tent were Han Solo, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], and a [[Phlog/Legends|Phlog]] named [[Haari Ikreme Beeerd]]. When Beeerd mocked the [[Podracing/Legends|podracing]] ability of Solo's friend Luke Skywalker, a full-scale brawl erupted. During the brawl, the Exodeenian threw a six-armed punch at Solo, but the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] avoided it by jumping away.<ref name="RFT" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Exodeenians were created by [[Kristine Kathryn Rusch]] for her novel ''[[The New Rebellion]]'', published by [[Bantam Spectra]] in [[1996]]. They received mentions in a number of reference books before another Exodeenian appeared in [[Alex Wheeler]]'s young adult reader ''[[Rebel Force: Target]]'', published by [[Scholastic]] in [[2008]]. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace|Dan Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] and published in [[2009]], provided a placement for their homeworld Exodeen, and included a number of historical maps, providing further background on the Exodeenians.

==Appearances==
